Conditions A to Z
Bronchiectasis 
Bronchiectasis is a condition where the airways in your lungs are damaged and produce a lot of phlegm (mucus). It cannot usually be cured, but treatment can help manage it.
Symptoms of bronchiectasis 
The main symptoms of bronchiectasis are:
a cough that does not go away coughing up a lot of phlegm (mucus) – the phlegm may be clear, white, yellow or green getting frequent chest infections, where your symptoms get worse for a few days or weeks and you may feel generally unwell Other symptoms can include:
wheezing shortness of breath noises when you breathe, such as crackling in your lungs, snoring sounds or high-pitched squeaks feeling very tired coughing up blood chest pain 
Information: 
Symptoms such as a cough or coughing up phlegm are common and can be caused by other conditions such as chest infections .
Non-urgent advice: See a GP if:
you think you could have bronchiectasis you've had a cough for more than 3 weeks 
Urgent advice: Ask for an urgent GP appointment or get help from NHS 111 if:
your cough is very bad or quickly gets worse – for example you're coughing up a lot of phlegm (mucus) every day you're coughing up
